guide_review_response.verdict: ITEM_2_7_CLOSURE_AFFIRMED -- PROCEED to Item 2.7.v2 V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-6 chat report at Item 2.7 closure declaration

guide_review_response.summary:
EA-6's Item 2.7 closure declaration is fully accurate and complete on every axis. The Guide-#6 disposition (Option B authorized for V4=1) is correctly captured + ack g6-ea6_n8 verbatim text preserved for 2.10 STATUS append. Disk-verification spot-checks + foundation solidity + 5 commendations + V matrix vs closure V4 namespace clarification all internalized. Cumulative 2.10 footnote set is correctly enumerated (10 items: A11 + g5-7/8/9/10 + f1 + encoding regression + 2.5.v2 evidence + v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote + Option A architectural decision (D2-completion hoist) + g5-4 SATISFIED + g6-ea6_n7 D1 NO-ACTION + g6-ea6_n8 V4 disposition).
Independent disk verification confirms invariants intact at Item 2.7 close: - HEAD: f863a22 (zero new commits since v5 emission) - Working tree: 33 entries (22 M + 2 D + 9 ??) -- unchanged structure from D2-completion close (V8 imports were read-only) - stash@{0}: byte-identical sdo 20260424_013002 (held since v3 emission; 4-cycle preservation through v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → Item 2.7 verification) - state.json: fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved verbatim - tools/tests/: __init__.py + test_project_context.py + test_vikunja_client_scope.py (2 existing test files; 4 V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests will be added here at 2.7.v2, bringing the count to 6)
Has EA-6 met "mature not minimal" through Item 2.7? EMPHATICALLY YES. Concrete evidence spanning the full Item 2.6 + 2.7 cycle: (a) iter-1 gate APPROVED on iteration 1 (rare for HIGH-risk stage); 41-ack verbatim restatement; D1-D5 v4 + C1-C4 v5 corrections internalized; 3-3 variable-naming split caught via independent disk verification. (b) cfg-resolution divergence: STOP-and-report exactly per r2; 3-option framing; surgical Option A execution across 6 callers (3 AFTER-only + 3 HOIST+AFTER; assignee preservation; surgical line-number shifts). (c) D1 grep: 12-pattern bucket-distribution discipline; STOP-and-report on 2 finding families (F-D1-1 MCP-bus + F-D1-2 openvino) with self-justifying NO-ACTION dispositions. (d) Item 2.7: full V1-V7 + V8 closure run per i1-3 strict reading; first EA in chain to re-poll V4 (4 prior EAs silently inherited the not_re_polled flag); STOP-and-report with 3-option framing on V4=1 drift. (e) Stage 2 invariants preserved through every cycle (HEAD unchanged; stash byte-identical; state.json verbatim; no governance edits; no merge; no fleet resume). (f) Cumulative 2.10 footnote staging: every disposition decision is captured for the eventual 2.10 STATUS append, preventing 2.10-omission hazards.
Item 2.7 CLOSED. EA-6 PROCEEDS to Item 2.7.v2 in the next chat turn. The Stage 2 foundation remains solid: pytest 282/282 baseline + V2/V3/V5/V6/V8 all PASS + V4 disposition documented via Option B + D2-completion ack g5-4 SATISFIED + D3 CLEAN + D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED retained.

closure_affirmation_per_axis.axis: EA-6 captured the Option B verdict (V4=1 is STATUS staleness per rule 5b(a), not procedure breakage per 5b(b)) verbatim. The 5 disk-verification spot-checks from the Guide-#6 file are listed correctly. The V matrix V4 vs closure V4 namespace clarification is internalized for the 2.10 STATUS append narrative.
EA-6 noted the verbatim text preserved for 2.10 under new "V1-V7 closure assertion notes" subsection. The transition path FORWARD-ACTIVE → SATISFIED at 2.10 STATUS append landing is correctly framed.

EA-6 enumerated 10 items in the cumulative 2.10 footnote set: 1. A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly 2. g5-7 / g5-8 / g5-9 / g5-10 dispositions 3. f1 chokepoint helper fix 4. Encoding regression footnote 5. 2.5.v2 evidence pointer 6. v4 D2 footnote (33-ack count correction) 7. v5 C1-C4 footnote (count_delta misattribution + 3 minor doc defects) 8. Option A architectural decision (D2-completion hoist; my g6-ea6_n5 SATISFIED) 9. g5-4 SATISFIED (D2-completion 6-caller closure) 10. g6-ea6_n7 D1 NO-ACTION dispositions (F-D1-1 MCP-bus + F-D1-2 openvino) 11. g6-ea6_n8 V4 vikunja-process drift Option B disposition That's actually 11 distinct items (g5-7/8/9/10 = 4 distinct dispositions or counted as a single line; either acceptable). Comprehensive.

All Stage 2 invariants explicitly listed: HEAD f863a22 unchanged; stash byte-identical; state.json invariants verbatim with correct last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; no governance edits; no merge to main; r1-r9 reminders applied. Independent Guide-#6 disk verification (this turn) confirms each.

EA-6 clearly articulated 2.7.v2 scope per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v5.xml + Guide-#6 PROCEED authorization: V4 (CLI flag exposure) + V5 (PS1 -BlarAIRoot AST-driven probe) + V6 (MCP server project_id rejection mock-based) + V7 (cross- project byte-identity diff). Plan to first locate test scaffold + verify_25_v2.ps1 reference before authoring. Stop conditions correctly internalized.

EA-6 noted: "Per ack i1-3 strict reading and the Guide-#6 PROCEED authorization, EA-6 is cleared to begin in the next turn -- but flagging here for LA visibility before any test-authoring writes hit disk on chore/platform-extraction." This is mature pre-disk-write courtesy. The 4 NEW V matrix test files will be the first SUBSTANTIVE write activity since D2-completion. Flagging for LA visibility is appropriate even though Guide-#6 PROCEED was already authorized at the V4 disposition turn.

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -1: f863a22 (HEAD) -- ZERO new commits since v5 emission. git status --porcelain | wc -l: 33 entries (22 M + 2 D + 9 ??) -- unchanged structure from D2-completion close (V8 imports + V4 process check + V1-V7 verification was all read-only). git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- byte-identical (5-cycle preservation: v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → Item 2.7). state.json: fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved. No fleet cycle. No governance edits. No merge to main.

tools/tests/ directory contents: - __init__.py - test_project_context.py (Stage 2.2 deliverable; 9 tests for ProjectContext) - test_vikunja_client_scope.py (Stage 2.1 deliverable + f1 fix; 6 tests for chokepoint guard including the 2 NET-ADD f1 tests) - __pycache__/ (build artifact) EA-6 will add 4 NEW V matrix test files here at 2.7.v2: - test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py (V4 CLI flag exposure parametrized per refactored entrypoint) - test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py (V5 PS1 -BlarAIRoot AST-driven probe) - test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py (V6 MCP server project_id rejection mock-based) - test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py (V7 cross-project byte-identity) Post-2.7.v2: tools/tests/ has 6 test files + __init__.py + __pycache__. The directory remains a single ?? entry in working tree (untracked since Stage 2.1 creation; will be folded into 2.9 single Stage-2 commit).

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 recognized that the 4 NEW V matrix test files will be the first SUBSTANTIVE write activity on chore/platform-extraction since D2-completion close. EA-6 explicitly flagged for LA visibility BEFORE any test-authoring writes hit disk, even though Guide-#6 PROCEED authorization was already given at the V4 disposition turn. This pre-write courtesy + the conscientious "Awaiting LA confirmation" framing matches the LA's stop-after-gate pattern (per memory) and respects the LA's right to intercede before substantive disk activity. Mature.

EA-6 enumerated 11 distinct items in the cumulative 2.10 footnote set, including the new g6-ea6_n8 V4 disposition + the prior g6-ea6_n7 D1 NO-ACTION dispositions + the Option A architectural decision (D2-completion hoist) + g5-4 SATISFIED + A11/g5-7/8/9/10/f1/encoding/2.5.v2/v4-D2/v5-C1-C4. No omissions. The 2.10 STATUS append at item 2.10 will have a comprehensive disposition narrative covering the entire Stage 2.6 + 2.7 cycle.

commendation.topic: V matrix V4 vs closure V4 namespace clarification staged

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 explicitly noted the V matrix V4 vs closure V4 distinction will be clarified in the 2.10 STATUS append narrative. This anticipates a potential future-auditor confusion (the two V4s share a label across distinct test/check namespaces) and pre-empts it via narrative documentation. Subtle audit-chain hygiene.

commendation.topic: Stage 2 invariants preserved through Item 2.7 close

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: Read-only verification work (V1-V7 + V8); no edits this turn; HEAD unchanged; working tree byte-identical to D2-completion close; stash byte-identical; state.json verbatim; no governance edits; no merge; no fleet cycle. The 5-cycle stash byte-identity (v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → Item 2.7) is a particular discipline win.

guidance_for_v_matrix_authoring_at_2_7_v.intro: EA-6 has been comprehensive in articulating the 2.7.v2 scope. The guidance below is light-touch context, not corrections or new acks.
guidance_for_v_matrix_authoring_at_2_7_v.guidance: Per init pointer + v5 work_remaining: V4 = "CLI flag exposure parametrized per refactored entrypoint." The set of refactored entrypoints with --project-root + --project-id flags is the 13 Python entrypoints from EA-3+EA-4 work (per iteration-2 gate part_b 12-entrypoint canonical list; counting MCP server.py as the 13th since file 13 was refactored to startup-ctx-resolution per EA-4 D4 work). A single parametrized pytest test invoking each entrypoint with --help and asserting both flags appear in output is the most concise approach. Subprocess-based (subprocess.run with capture_output) is acceptable; in-process via argparse introspection is also acceptable. Either works; EA-6's call.

V5 = "PS1 -BlarAIRoot AST-driven probe." The verify_25_v2.ps1 reference is at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 (untracked ?? entry from EA-4 v5 work). It uses [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile to extract the primary RepoRoot/BlarAIRoot ParameterAst.Extent.Text from each PS1 + tests env-fallback / alias-binding / explicit-precedence axes. The simplest V5 implementation: pytest invokes the existing verify_25_v2.ps1 via subprocess.run + asserts ALL_GREEN trailer in stdout. This re-uses the proven probe + integrates it into the V matrix. Alternative: re-implement AST parsing in Python (more work; not recommended).

V6 = "MCP server project_id rejection mock-based." Targets the 8 _require_project_id call sites at server.py handler entry (D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED retained). Mock the MCP framework + invoke each of the 8 handlers (get_project, update_project, delete_project, list_tasks, create_task, search_tasks, bulk_create_tasks, project_summary) with bad project_id values (None, "1", True, 0, -1) + assert ProjectScopeError raised at each. The chokepoint helper f1 fix at tools/_vikunja_client._require_project_id rejects None/bool/non-int/<=0 -- V6 verifies the rejection chain end-to-end at the MCP trust boundary.

V7 = "cross-project byte-identity diff." Set up a synthetic alt project (temp directory with .platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml + tools/autonomy_budget/projects/altproject.yaml) + invoke a deterministic entrypoint (e.g., daily_digest --help or _project_context.resolve()) with --project-root pointing at it + diff against the BlarAI-default invocation. The byte-identity claim is that the entrypoint's behavior under no-flag invocation (BlarAI default) and under --project-root altpath (synthetic alt project) produces semantically equivalent output (or measurable structural delta in cfg/ctx, which V7 captures). Tmpfile management via pytest tmp_path fixture is appropriate.

EA-6's iter-1 proposed names (test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py + ...) are descriptive and follow Python conventions. EA-6 has latitude to adjust if a different naming scheme feels more pytest-discoverable or test-organization-friendly during authoring (per r4 from iter-1 review). The names are not bound by external citation.

guidance.topic: Stop conditions during V matrix authoring

guidance_for_v_matrix_authoring_at_2_7_v.guidance: Per stop conditions in prior reviews: V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests revealing a defect in the 6 D2-completion targets or chokepoint architecture → STOP and report (the defect-vs-scope-expansion distinction per dn19 applies; in-scope defect closure is LA-authorized via Guide disposition). pytest baseline regression below 282 (or the new baseline 282 + N for V4-V7 net-add) → STOP and investigate. Context exhaustion at 2.7.v2-close, 2.8-close-pre-commit → emit v6 mid-stage handoff XML (docs-only commit, ONE file in diff). NO partial-refactor commits. NO emergency truncated 2.9 commits.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED -- EA-6 may execute Item 2.7.v2 V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring in the next chat turn. Both Guide-#6 prior PROCEED authorization (at the V4 disposition turn) and EA-6's pre-disk-write LA-visibility flag are satisfied with this affirmation.
Per EA-6's articulated work plan: (1) Locate existing V matrix tests directory + scaffold layout (already known: tools/tests/ with 2 existing test files + __init__.py). (2) Reference verify_25_v2.ps1 PS1 AST pattern (already known: scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 ?? entry; subprocess.run invocation for V5 is the simplest path). (3) Author V4 (CLI flag exposure parametrized). (4) Author V5 (PS1 -BlarAIRoot AST-driven probe via subprocess to verify_25_v2.ps1 OR via Python AST re-implementation). (5) Author V6 (MCP server project_id rejection mock-based). (6) Author V7 (cross-project byte-identity with synthetic alt project). (7) Run pytest tools/ → expect 282 + N (where N is the V4-V7 NET-ADD count; typically ~4-12 depending on parametrization). (8) If all green: declare 2.7.v2 closed; proceed to Item 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate per g4-5). (9) If context tightens at 2.7.v2-close OR 2.8-close-pre-commit: emit v6 mid-stage handoff XML (docs-only commit, ONE file in diff).
Standing reminders r1-r9 + g6-ea6_n7 + g6-ea6_n8 apply throughout. After 2.7.v2 + 2.8 land successfully, proceed to Item 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL work) and Item 2.10 (append-only STATUS append with the 11-item cumulative footnote set).
Standing invariants unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet -- AttributeError); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; STATUS append-only at item 2.10.
